test_cases.description: walk_response_schema returns FieldEntry for each leaf in a flat object schema
test_cases.input_description: Flat schema with string, integer, boolean properties
test_cases.expected_output_description: WalkResult with 3 FieldEntry items, no warnings

test_cases.description: walk_response_schema traverses nested objects and produces dot-separated paths
test_cases.input_description: Schema with nested object containing leaf fields
assertions: any('user.name' in f.path for f in result.fields)

test_cases.description: walk_response_schema resolves $ref via ref_resolver and discovers fields in referenced schema
test_cases.input_description: Schema with $ref pointing to another schema with leaf fields
test_cases.expected_output_description: Fields from referenced schema appear in result

test_cases.description: Empty/None schema produces INCOMPLETE_SCHEMA warning
assertions: any(w.code == FindingCode.INCOMPLETE_SCHEMA for w in result.warnings)

test_cases.description: Circular $ref chains are detected and emitted as INCOMPLETE_SCHEMA warnings without infinite recursion
test_cases.input_description: Schema where A refs B refs A
test_cases.expected_output_description: INCOMPLETE_SCHEMA warning for cycle, no infinite recursion
assertions: any(w.code == FindingCode.INCOMPLETE_SCHEMA for w in result.warnings)

test_cases.description: Traversal exceeding max_depth raises max_depth_exceeded error
assertions: exception is raised for depth exceeded

test_cases.description: ref_resolver raising exception causes ref_resolver_failure error
test_cases.input_description: Schema with $ref and a resolver that raises
assertions: exception is raised when resolver fails

test_cases.description: Non-dict schema raises invalid_schema_type error
test_cases.input_description: Schema passed as a list instead of dict
assertions: exception is raised for non-dict schema

test_cases.description: walk_response_schema flattens allOf with union semantics
test_cases.input_description: Schema with allOf containing two object schemas
test_cases.expected_output_description: Fields from both sub-schemas appear in result

test_cases.description: walk_response_schema traverses array items to discover leaf fields
test_cases.input_description: Schema with type=array and items with properties
test_cases.expected_output_description: Fields from array items appear in result

test_cases.description: Nullable fields are correctly recorded in FieldEntry
test_cases.input_description: Schema with a nullable string field

test_cases.description: Result contains only leaf fields, no intermediate object/array nodes
test_cases.input_description: Schema with nested objects and arrays
assertions: all(f.field_type not in ('object', 'array') for f in result.fields)

test_cases.description: Same inputs always produce same outputs (pure function)
classify_fields matches fields using fnmatch pattern and assigns correct tier
test_cases.input_description: FieldEntry with path 'response.user.email', registry with fnmatch pattern '*email*' -> CONFIDENTIAL

test_cases.description: classify_fields matches fields using regex pattern
test_cases.input_description: FieldEntry with path 'response.user.ssn', registry with regex '.*ssn$' -> RESTRICTED

test_cases.description: Fields matching no pattern are assigned DataTier.PUBLIC
test_cases.input_description: FieldEntry with path 'response.status', no matching registry entries

test_cases.description: When field matches multiple patterns, highest DataTier wins
test_cases.input_description: FieldEntry matching both INTERNAL and RESTRICTED patterns

test_cases.description: Every input field appears exactly once in classified_fields
tier_set equals frozenset of all classified field tiers
test_cases.input_description: Fields matching CONFIDENTIAL and PUBLIC patterns
test_cases.expected_output_description: tier_set contains exactly those two tiers

test_cases.description: Invalid regex pattern raises invalid_regex_pattern error
test_cases.input_description: Registry entry with pattern_type='regex' and pattern '[invalid'

test_cases.description: FieldEntry with empty path raises empty_field_path error
Same inputs produce same outputs (pure function)
test_cases.input_description: Same fields and registry called twice

test_cases.description: compute_structural_profile composes walk and classify to produce valid profile
test_cases.input_description: Valid node_id, adapter_slot_id, endpoint, schema with leaf fields, registry entries
test_cases.expected_output_description: StructuralProfile with correct ids, tiers, classified_fields, UTC timestamp

test_cases.description: Empty schema with assume_worst_on_incomplete=True injects RESTRICTED tier
test_cases.input_description: Empty schema dict, gate_config with assume_worst_on_incomplete=True

test_cases.description: schema_complete is True when no warnings exist
computed_at is a valid ISO 8601 UTC timestamp
assertions: computed_at ends with '+00:00' or 'Z' or contains timezone info

test_cases.description: classified_fields tiers are consistent with result.tiers
test_cases.input_description: Schema with fields matching multiple tiers
test_cases.expected_output_description: All tiers from classified_fields appear in result.tiers

test_cases.description: Empty node_id violates precondition
audit_slot returns ALLOW when all structural tiers are declared
test_cases.input_description: Profile with tiers=[PUBLIC, INTERNAL], declared_read_tiers=[PUBLIC, INTERNAL]
test_cases.expected_output_description: SlotDecision with decision=ALLOW, no C005 findings
assertions: not any(f.code == FindingCode.C005 for f in result.findings)

test_cases.description: audit_slot returns BLOCK when undeclared tiers exist and C005 in block_on_codes
test_cases.expected_output_description: SlotDecision with decision=BLOCK, C005 finding present
assertions: any(f.code == FindingCode.C005 for f in result.findings)

test_cases.description: Exactly one C005 finding per undeclared tier
test_cases.input_description: Profile with tiers=[PUBLIC, CONFIDENTIAL, RESTRICTED], declared=[PUBLIC]
assertions: sum(1 for f in result.findings if f.code == FindingCode.C005) == 2

test_cases.description: All finding messages include the specific node_id
assertions: all(node_id in f.message for f in result.findings)

test_cases.description: C005 findings have severity >= WARNING
assertions: all(f.severity.value >= FindingSeverity.WARNING.value for f in result.findings if f.code == FindingCode.C005)

test_cases.description: Mismatched node_ids between profile and declared_access raises error
BLOCK iff at least one finding code is in block_on_codes
test_cases.input_description: Undeclared tiers but C005 not in block_on_codes
test_cases.expected_output_description: ALLOW even though C005 findings exist

test_cases.description: decided_at is a valid ISO 8601 UTC timestamp
result.profile is the same profile passed in
INCOMPLETE_SCHEMA warnings from profile appear as findings
assertions: any(f.code == FindingCode.INCOMPLETE_SCHEMA for f in result.findings)

test_cases.description: No findings when observed tiers are subset of declared tiers
FA_A_015 finding for each observed tier not in declared
FA_A_015 findings have severity >= ERROR
assertions: all(f.severity.value >= FindingSeverity.ERROR.value for f in result)

test_cases.description: Finding messages include node_id and undeclared tier
assertions: all(node_id in f.message for f in result)

test_cases.description: Mismatched node_ids raises node_id_mismatch error
One FA_A_015 per undeclared tier when multiple tiers are undeclared
assertions: all(f.code == FindingCode.FA_A_015 for f in result)

test_cases.description: Each finding timestamp >= observed.observed_at
load_gate_config with empty dict returns sensible defaults
load_gate_config with explicit values uses those values
Invalid finding code in block_on_codes raises error
Non-dict config_source raises error
Missing block_on_codes key defaults to [C005]
All values in result.block_on_codes are valid FindingCode variants
test_cases.expected_output_description: All codes are FindingCode enum members
assertions: all(isinstance(c, FindingCode) for c in result.block_on_codes)

test_cases.description: load_classification_registry loads valid entries
test_cases.input_description: List of valid registry dicts with fnmatch and regex
test_cases.expected_output_description: List of ClassificationRegistryEntry of same length

test_cases.description: Invalid tier value raises invalid_tier error
Invalid regex raises invalid_regex error
Missing required key raises missing_required_key error
Invalid pattern_type raises invalid_pattern_type error
assertions: exception raised for invalid pattern type

test_cases.description: All tier values in result are valid DataTier variants
assertions: all(isinstance(e.tier, DataTier) for e in result)

test_cases.description: All pattern_type values are fnmatch or regex
assertions: all(e.pattern_type in ('fnmatch', 'regex') for e in result)

test_cases.description: DataTier ordering is PUBLIC < INTERNAL < CONFIDENTIAL < RESTRICTED
Classification of unmatched fields defaults to PUBLIC
FindingCode C005 and FA_A_015 are stable string identifiers
test_cases.expected_output_description: C005 value is 'C005', FA_A_015 value is 'FA_A_015'

test_cases.description: End-to-end: schema with only PUBLIC fields and matching declarations yields ALLOW
test_cases.input_description: Simple schema, PUBLIC fields, declared PUBLIC read

test_cases.description: End-to-end: schema with CONFIDENTIAL fields undeclared yields BLOCK
test_cases.input_description: Schema with email field, CONFIDENTIAL pattern, only PUBLIC declared
assertions: any(f.code == FindingCode.C005 for f in decision.findings)

test_cases.description: End-to-end: empty schema with assume_worst triggers RESTRICTED and BLOCK
test_cases.input_description: Empty schema, assume_worst=True, only PUBLIC declared
